headline: ONE SUPPLIER OR TWO?
subheading: Use ICH Q9 risk thinking to weigh supply continuity, qualification cost and patient safety.
flux_prompt: Abstract editorial illustration of pharmaceutical supply-chain resilience and sourcing strategy, set in a deep navy environment. Two parallel flowing supply lines of softly glowing teal and cyan light streams travel left to right across the composition, evoking a primary supply route and a redundant backup route; one line continues unbroken while the other branches and rejoins, suggesting dual sourcing redundancy versus a single point of failure. Translucent frosted-glass vial silhouettes and abstract geometric supply nodes float along the streams as smooth bokeh shapes, with a single subtle green accent node marking a qualified second source. Faint interconnecting network lines and a delicate risk-matrix grid texture dissolve into the dark background, hinting at quality risk management without any readable symbols. Polished surfaces, soft volumetric light, shallow depth of field, condensation-like sparkle highlights, clinical-luxury aesthetic, premium minimal corporate mood, cinematic gradient from deep navy to teal. 1500x900 composition, 5:3 aspect ratio, professional editorial illustration, premium quality, clean composition, modern visual storytelling, professional lighting, no text, no watermark, no logo, ultra detailed
